sentences: I had to hit the ground running this morning, as I'm usually a creature of habit and don't like sudden changes to my routine.
After an incredibly packed day, I was more than ready to call it a night and just unwind with a good book.

newWords.meaning: 어떤 일을 시작하자마자 에너지를 쏟아붓거나 빠르게 진행하다.
newWords.context: I had to **hit the ground running** this morning

newWords.meaning: 습관대로 행동하는 사람, 루틴을 중요하게 생각하는 사람.
newWords.context: I'm usually **a creature of habit** and don't like sudden changes

newWords.meaning: (밤에) 일을 멈추고 집에 가거나 잠자리에 들다.
newWords.context: I was more than ready to **call it a night**

sentences: With deadlines looming, I've really had a lot on my plate this week, constantly burning the midnight oil to get everything done.
I'm hoping to get a handle on my schedule next week and finally catch some Z's over the weekend.

newWords.word: have a lot on one's plate
newWords.meaning: 할 일이 아주 많다, 바쁘다 (처리해야 할 업무나 책임이 많아 매우 바쁘다는 의미)
newWords.context: I've really had a lot on my plate this week

newWords.meaning: 밤늦게까지 일하다/공부하다, 밤샘하다
newWords.context: constantly burning the midnight oil to get everything done

newWords.meaning: ~을 통제하다/관리하다/이해하다 (상황이나 문제 등을 파악하고 해결할 수 있게 되다)
newWords.context: to get a handle on my schedule next week

sentences: I was feeling under the weather after pulling an all-nighter.
I decided to call it a day and just rest.

newWords.meaning: 몸 상태가 좋지 않은
newWords.context: I was feeling under the weather

newWords.meaning: 밤을 새우다
newWords.context: after pulling an all-nighter

newWords.meaning: 오늘은 여기까지 하다, 일과를 마치다
newWords.context: I decided to call it a day

sentences: Learning a new language is no piece of cake.
But if you stick to your guns, you'll eventually get the hang of it.

newWords.meaning: 식은 죽 먹기, 아주 쉬운 일
newWords.context: Language is no piece of cake

newWords.meaning: 너의 뜻을(주장을) 굽히지 않다, 밀어붙이다
newWords.context: if you stick to your guns

newWords.meaning: 감(요령)을 잡다
newWords.context: you'll eventually get the hang of it

sentences: To truly move the needle on this project, we need to stop cutting corners and invest in proper resources.
Otherwise, we'll find ourselves back to the drawing board next quarter, having wasted valuable time.

newWords.meaning: 성과를 내다, 큰 변화를 일으키다
newWords.context: To truly **move the needle** on this project

newWords.meaning: 절차를 무시하고 대충 하다, 편법을 쓰다 (종종 품질 저하를 동반함)
newWords.context: stop **cutting corners** and invest

newWords.meaning: 원점으로 돌아가 다시 시작하다 (기존 계획이 실패했을 때)
newWords.context: find ourselves **back to the drawing board**

sentences: After months of incremental efforts, the CEO urged us to stop just **spinning our wheels** and instead focus on initiatives that truly **move the needle** on profitability.
This calls for some bold, **blue-sky thinking** from every department, rather than just settling for the usual quick wins.

newWords.meaning: 공회전하다, 헛돌다 (많은 노력에도 불구하고 진전이 없음을 의미)
newWords.context: stop just spinning our wheels

newWords.meaning: (상황에) 큰 변화를 주다, 상당한 진전을 이루다
newWords.context: truly move the needle on profitability

newWords.meaning: 제약 없는 자유로운 사고, (현실성이 떨어지더라도) 창의적인 발상
newWords.context: some bold, blue-sky thinking

sentences: We need to think outside the box to beat our competitors.
Let's touch base next week to review the new strategy.

newWords.meaning: 고정관념에서 벗어나 창의적으로 생각하다
newWords.context: We need to think outside the box

newWords.meaning: 연락하다, 상황을 확인하다
newWords.context: Let's touch base next week

sentences: The manager gave us the green light to start the project.
However, she warned us to keep our eyes peeled for any budget issues by the end of the month.

newWords.meaning: 승인, 허가
newWords.context: manager gave us the green light

newWords.meaning: 눈을 부릅뜨고 경계하다, 주의를 기울이다
newWords.context: warned us to keep our eyes peeled

sentences: Tired of the usual tourist traps, I'm planning my next trip to truly get off the beaten path and immerse myself in communities where I can really soak up the local flavour.
I'm even prepared to rough it a bit by staying in hostels or camping, as long as it means a more authentic and enriching cultural experience.

newWords.meaning: 관광객이 잘 가지 않는 곳으로 가다, 남들이 잘 안 가는 길을 가다
newWords.context: to truly get off the beaten path and immerse myself

newWords.meaning: 현지 분위기(색채/정서)를 흠뻑 느끼다/흡수하다
newWords.context: can really soak up the local flavour

newWords.meaning: 불편하게 지내다, (고생을 감수하고) 생활하다
newWords.context: prepared to rough it a bit by staying in hostels

sentences: After months cooped up in the office, I've really started to get itchy feet, so I'm planning a backpacking trip soon.
This time, I'm determined to go somewhere completely off the beaten path and rough it a bit, rather than sticking to crowded tourist hubs.

newWords.meaning: 여행을 가고 싶어 몸이 근질거리다, 한곳에 오래 머물지 못하고 돌아다니고 싶어 하다
newWords.context: started to get itchy feet

newWords.meaning: 잘 알려지지 않은 곳, 일반적인 경로를 벗어난 곳
newWords.context: somewhere completely off the beaten path

newWords.meaning: 불편한 환경에서 지내다, 야영하다, 검소하게 살다
newWords.context: and rough it a bit

sentences: We decided to hit the road bright and early to avoid traffic.
We traveled light so we wouldn't have to wait for baggage claim.

newWords.meaning: 여행을 떠나다, 출발하다
newWords.context: decided to hit the road bright and early

newWords.meaning: 짐을 가볍게 하여 여행하다
newWords.context: We traveled light

sentences: Having recently picked up golf, my friend Mark really took to it like a duck to water, surprising everyone with his natural swing and quick mastery of the game's nuances.
He's already talking about entering local tournaments, and I wouldn't be surprised if this newfound passion turns out to be a real game changer for his future.

newWords.word: take to something like a duck to water
newWords.meaning: (새로운 활동 등에) 물 만난 고기처럼 능숙하게 해내다; 천성적으로 잘하다
newWords.context: Mark really took to it like a duck to water

newWords.meaning: 판도를 바꾸는 것, 상황을 완전히 뒤바꾸는 요인/사건
newWords.context: a real game changer for his future

sentences: Despite the early setbacks, the amateur climbing team is determined to hit the ground running as they prepare for the national competition, willing to go the extra mile in every training session.
The final round of the esports tournament was an absolute thriller, keeping everyone on the edge of their seats as the match went right down to the wire.

newWords.meaning: (새로운 일을) 즉시 성공적으로 시작하다, 순조롭게 출발하다
newWords.context: determined to hit the ground running as they prepare

newWords.meaning: 특별한 노력을 기울이다, 한층 더 노력하다
newWords.context: willing to go the extra mile in every training session

newWords.meaning: 막판까지 가는 접전, 마지막 순간까지 승부가 결정되지 않는
newWords.context: as the match went right down to the wire

sentences: John was on the fence about joining the soccer league this year.
He finally decided to bite the bullet and sign up.

newWords.meaning: 결정하지 못하고 망설이는 (미정인)
newWords.context: John was on the fence about joining

newWords.meaning: 하기 싫거나 피하기 힘든 일을 이 꽉 물고 하다
newWords.context: He finally decided to bite the bullet

sentences: The team was down by two goals and threw in the towel early.
They really dropped the ball during the second half.

newWords.meaning: (수건을 던지다) 포기하다, 항복하다
newWords.context: threw in the towel early

newWords.meaning: 실수하다, 책임을 다하지 못하다
newWords.context: They really dropped the ball
